questions.stem: Which of the following transactions is within the scope of ASC 718 (Compensation — Stock Compensation)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A company issues shares to acquire a business in a business combination

choices.label: B
choices.text: A company grants stock options to employees as part of their compensation package

choices.label: C
choices.text: A company issues shares in an initial public offering

choices.label: D
choices.text: A company repurchases its own shares on the open market

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-15 applies to all share-based payment transactions in which an entity acquires goods or services by issuing (or offering to issue) its shares, stock options, or other equity instruments. Employee stock option grants are the most common example. Business combinations (A) are covered by ASC 805, IPOs (C) are equity transactions, and share repurchases (D) are treasury stock transactions under ASC 505.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-15は、企業が株式・ストックオプション等の持分商品を発行して財・サービスを取得する全ての株式報酬取引に適用される。従業員へのストックオプション付与が典型例。企業結合(A)はASC 805、IPO(C)は資本取引、自社株買い(D)はASC 505の範囲。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Measurement at Grant Date
questions.stem: Under ASC 718, when should the fair value of a stock option granted to an employee be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the vesting date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the exercise date

choices.label: D
choices.text: At each reporting date until exercise

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires that the fair value of equity-classified stock options be measured at the grant date — the date at which the employer and employee reach a mutual understanding of the key terms and conditions of the award. This grant-date fair value is then used to recognize compensation expense over the requisite service period. The fair value is not subsequently remeasured for equity-classified awards.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2により、持分分類のストックオプションの公正価値は付与日（grant date）に測定する。付与日とは雇用者と従業員が報酬の主要条件について合意した日である。この公正価値を必要勤務期間にわたり報酬費用として認識する。持分分類の場合、その後の再測定は行わない。

questions.stem: A company grants 10,000 stock options to an employee on January 1, Year 1. The options have a grant-date fair value of $8 per option and vest over a 4-year service period (cliff vesting). What is the compensation expense recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 10,000 options × $8 = $80,000. Under ASC 718-10-25-1, compensation cost is recognized on a straight-line basis over the requisite service period for cliff-vesting awards. Year 1 expense = $80,000 / 4 years = $20,000. The expense is recognized regardless of whether the options are ultimately exercised.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬総額 = 10,000個 × $8 = $80,000。崖式権利確定(cliff vesting)の場合、ASC 718-10-25-1に基づき必要勤務期間にわたり定額法で費用認識。Year 1費用 = $80,000 ÷ 4年 = $20,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a market condition under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The employee must remain employed for three years

choices.label: B
choices.text: The company must achieve earnings per share of $5.00

choices.label: C
choices.text: The company's stock price must reach $50 per share

choices.label: D
choices.text: The employee must complete a specific project

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-20 defines a market condition as a condition upon which the exercise price, exercisability, or other pertinent factors depend that is related to the achievement of a specified stock price or a specified amount of intrinsic value. A target stock price of $50 (C) is a market condition. Continued employment (A) is a service condition. EPS target (B) is a performance condition. Project completion (D) is also a performance condition.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-20では、市場条件を株価や本質的価値の達成に関連する条件と定義する。目標株価$50(C)が市場条件。継続雇用(A)は勤務条件、EPS目標(B)は業績条件、プロジェクト完了(D)も業績条件。

questions.stem: A company grants stock options with an exercise price of $30 when the stock price is $30. The fair value of each option using an option-pricing model is $12. Under ASC 718, what amount per option is used to measure compensation cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $12 (fair value)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $0 (intrinsic value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30 (stock price)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $42 (stock price plus fair value)

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires the use of fair value to measure compensation cost for stock options, not intrinsic value. Even when options are granted at-the-money (exercise price equals stock price, making intrinsic value $0), the fair value from an option-pricing model captures the time value and expected volatility. The $12 fair value per option is used to determine total compensation expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2は、ストックオプションの報酬費用測定に公正価値を使用することを要求する。アットザマネー（行使価格=株価、本源的価値$0）であっても、オプション価格モデルによる公正価値$12が時間的価値とボラティリティを反映する。

questions.stem: A company initially estimates that 10% of stock options will be forfeited. In Year 2, the company revises this estimate to 15%. How should the change in estimated forfeitures be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospectively, by adjusting expense in future periods only

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a cumulative catch-up adjustment in the period of the change

choices.label: C
choices.text: By restating prior period financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an extraordinary item in the income statement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-3, changes in estimated forfeitures are accounted for as a change in estimate, which results in a cumulative catch-up adjustment in the period the estimate is revised. The cumulative compensation cost recognized to date is adjusted to reflect the revised forfeiture estimate, with the difference recognized in the current period. Prior periods are not restated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-3により、失効見積りの変更は見積りの変更として処理し、変更期間に累積的キャッチアップ調整を行う。当期までの累積報酬費用を修正後の失効率で再計算し、差額を当期に認識。過年度の修正再表示は行わない。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT an input to the Black-Scholes option-pricing model used to estimate the fair value of stock options under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expected term of the option

choices.label: B
choices.text: Risk-free interest rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected forfeiture rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expected volatility of the underlying stock

questions.explanation: The Black-Scholes model inputs include: (1) exercise price, (2) current stock price, (3) expected term, (4) expected volatility, (5) risk-free interest rate, and (6) expected dividends. The expected forfeiture rate is NOT an input to the option-pricing model itself. Forfeitures are accounted for separately by adjusting the number of awards expected to vest, not by adjusting the per-option fair value.
questions.explanationJa: Black-Scholesモデルの入力変数は、行使価格、株価、予想期間、予想ボラティリティ、リスクフリー金利、予想配当の6つ。予想失効率はモデルの入力変数ではなく、権利確定見込数の調整として別途反映する。

questions.stem: A company grants 9,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 with a fair value of $10 per option. The options vest in three equal annual installments (3,000 each year). Using the straight-line method for each tranche, what is the total compensation expense in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under graded vesting with separate tranches: Tranche 1 (3,000 options, 1-year vest): $30,000 / 1 = $30,000 in Year 1. Tranche 2 (3,000 options, 2-year vest): $30,000 / 2 = $15,000 in Year 1. Tranche 3 (3,000 options, 3-year vest): $30,000 / 3 = $10,000 in Year 1. Total Year 1 = $30,000 + $15,000 + $10,000 = $55,000. Alternatively, using straight-line over the total award: $90,000 / 3 = $30,000. The accelerated method gives $50,000 when calculated as each tranche recognized over its specific vesting period. Year 1 total = $50,000.
questions.explanationJa: 段階的権利確定をトランチ別に処理: 第1トランチ(3,000個/1年) Year 1: $30,000。第2トランチ(3,000個/2年) Year 1: $15,000。第3トランチ(3,000個/3年) Year 1: $10,000 ではなく、加速法による配分で合計$50,000。各トランチの費用を個別の権利確定期間で認識する。

questions.stem: A company grants 5,000 stock options contingent on achieving cumulative revenue of $10 million over 3 years. At the end of Year 1, management assesses the performance condition as probable of being met. The grant-date fair value is $6 per option. What is the cumulative compensation expense recognized through Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, compensation cost for awards with performance conditions is recognized only when the achievement of the condition is probable. Since management assesses the condition as probable at Year 1 end, cumulative expense through Year 1 = (5,000 × $6) × (1/3) = $10,000. If the condition were not probable, no expense would be recognized until it becomes probable, at which point a cumulative catch-up would be required.
questions.explanationJa: 業績条件付き報酬は、条件達成が「probable（蓋然性が高い）」と判断された場合にのみ費用認識する。Year 1末に達成見込みと判断したため、累積費用 = (5,000 × $6) × 1/3 = $10,000。達成見込みなしと判断されれば費用ゼロ。

questions.stem: A company grants stock options that vest only if the stock price reaches $75 within 4 years. The grant-date fair value, which incorporates the market condition using a Monte Carlo simulation, is $5 per option. After 3 years, it becomes clear the stock price target will not be met. What is the effect on compensation expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All previously recognized expense is reversed

choices.label: B
choices.text: Compensation expense continues to be recognized over the remaining service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense recognition stops immediately, but previously recognized expense is not reversed

choices.label: D
choices.text: An impairment loss is recorded

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, a market condition is reflected in the grant-date fair value estimate (using a lattice or Monte Carlo model), and compensation cost is recognized over the requisite service period regardless of whether the market condition is met. Unlike performance conditions, the outcome of a market condition does not affect whether compensation cost is recognized — only the service condition matters. Expense is never reversed for market condition failure.
questions.explanationJa: 市場条件は付与日公正価値の算定に反映される（モンテカルロ等）。ASC 718-10-25-20により、市場条件の達成・未達にかかわらず、必要勤務期間にわたり報酬費用を認識し続ける。業績条件と異なり、市場条件未達でも費用の取消しは行わない。

questions.stem: A company grants cash-settled stock appreciation rights (SARs) to employees. How should the compensation expense be measured at each reporting date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the grant-date fair value, with no subsequent remeasurement

choices.label: B
choices.text: At intrinsic value only at the settlement date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At fair value at each reporting date until settlement, with changes recognized in income

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the lower of grant-date fair value or current fair value

questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are classified as liabilities under ASC 718-30-25. Liability-classified awards are remeasured at fair value at each reporting date until settlement, with changes in fair value recognized as compensation expense (or credit) in the income statement. This contrasts with equity-classified awards, which are measured once at the grant date and not subsequently remeasured.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済型SARはASC 718-30-25に基づき負債に分類される。負債分類の報酬は決済まで各報告日に公正価値で再測定し、変動額を損益（報酬費用）として認識する。持分分類の報酬（付与日に一度だけ測定）とは対照的。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset from Stock Compensation
questions.stem: A company recognizes $200,000 of cumulative stock compensation expense for book purposes by Year 2. The tax deduction will occur at exercise. Assuming a 25% tax rate and the award is equity-classified, what deferred tax asset is recognized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-740-25, a deferred tax asset (DTA) is recognized based on the book compensation expense. DTA = cumulative book expense × tax rate = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. The tax deduction does not occur until the options are exercised (for NQSOs) or shares are sold (for ISOs with a disqualifying disposition). The temporary difference creates a DTA that will reverse upon exercise.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-740-25により、帳簿上の報酬費用に基づき繰延税金資産(DTA)を認識する。DTA = 累積帳簿費用 × 税率 = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。税務上の控除はオプション行使時（NQSO）まで発生しないため、一時差異として繰延税金資産を計上。

questions.stem: A company modifies stock options by reducing the exercise price from $40 to $30. Immediately before the modification, the fair value of the original options is $8 per option. Immediately after, the fair value of the modified options is $14 per option. What is the incremental compensation cost per option?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, when equity-classified awards are modified, the incremental compensation cost is measured as the excess of the fair value of the modified award over the fair value of the original award immediately before the modification. Incremental cost = $14 - $8 = $6 per option. The original grant-date fair value continues to be recognized, plus the incremental $6 is recognized over the remaining service period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、持分分類の報酬を変更した場合の追加報酬費用は、変更直前の原報酬の公正価値と変更後の公正価値の差額で測定する。追加費用 = $14 - $8 = $6/個。原報酬の付与日公正価値の認識は継続し、追加$6を残存勤務期間で認識。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company grants 20,000 stock options with a fair value of $15 per option. The options cliff-vest after 3 years of service. Estimated forfeitures are 5%. What is the compensation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 20,000 × $15 × (1 - 5%) = $285,000. Straight-line over 3 years: annual expense = $285,000 / 3 = $95,000. Year 2 expense is $95,000, the same as Year 1 and Year 3 (assuming no change in the forfeiture estimate). Each year recognizes one-third of the total expected cost.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬総額 = 20,000 × $15 × (1 - 5%) = $285,000。崖式3年確定で定額法: 年間費用 = $285,000 ÷ 3 = $95,000。失効見積りに変更がなければ、Year 2の費用は$95,000（毎年同額）。

questions.stem: Which of the following stock-based payment arrangements should be classified as a liability rather than equity under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stock options that the employee can only exercise by purchasing shares at the exercise price

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restricted stock units that settle in shares upon vesting

choices.label: C
choices.text: Stock appreciation rights that will be settled in cash at the employee's election

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restricted stock with a service condition only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-15, awards that will or may be settled in cash are classified as liabilities. Cash-settled SARs (where the employee receives cash equal to the stock price appreciation) are classified as liabilities because the entity has an obligation to transfer cash. Equity-settled instruments — standard stock options (A), share-settled RSUs (B), and service-condition restricted stock (D) — are classified as equity.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-15により、現金で決済される（またはされうる）報酬は負債に分類する。現金決済型SAR(C)は株価上昇分を現金で支払う義務があるため負債。通常のストックオプション(A)、株式決済RSU(B)、勤務条件のみの制限付株式(D)は持分に分類。

questions.stem: A company grants performance-based stock options. During Year 2, the company modifies the performance target, making achievement more likely. Before modification, achievement was deemed improbable (no expense recognized). After modification, achievement is probable. The original award fair value was $100,000 and the modified fair value is $120,000. What is the cumulative catch-up expense at the modification date (end of Year 2 of a 3-year service period)?
questions.explanation: When a modification changes a performance condition from improbable to probable, the entity must recognize a cumulative catch-up. The total cost is the greater of: (1) original grant-date fair value ($100,000) or (2) modified fair value ($120,000). Since the modified value is higher, total cost = $120,000. Cumulative expense through Year 2 (2/3 of service period) = $120,000 × 2/3 = $80,000. Since no prior expense was recognized ($0), the full $80,000 is caught up at the modification date.
questions.explanationJa: 業績条件が「達成見込みなし」から「達成見込みあり」に変更された場合、累積キャッチアップが必要。報酬総額は原報酬$100,000と変更後$120,000の大きい方 = $120,000。Year 2末までの累積費用 = $120,000 × 2/3 = $80,000。従前認識額$0のため、$80,000全額をキャッチアップ。

questions.stem: A company recognized $300,000 of cumulative compensation expense for NQSOs. At exercise, the actual tax deduction is $400,000. The tax rate is 25%. What is the excess tax benefit recognized, and where is it reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $25,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $100,000, recognized in additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: $25,000, recognized in additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: $100,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-740-35 (post ASU 2016-09), all excess tax benefits (and deficiencies) are recognized in the income statement as a component of income tax expense. DTA based on book expense = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000. Actual tax benefit = $400,000 × 25% = $100,000. Excess tax benefit = $100,000 - $75,000 = $25,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense. Prior to ASU 2016-09, excess benefits went to APIC.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09以降、超過税務便益は全額損益計算書の法人税等で認識する。帳簿ベースDTA = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000。実際税務便益 = $400,000 × 25% = $100,000。超過分 = $100,000 - $75,000 = $25,000を法人税等の減少として認識。ASU 2016-09以前はAPICに計上していた。

questions.stem: A company grants equity-settled SARs with a grant-date fair value of $50,000 that vest over 2 years. At the end of Year 1, the fair value of the SARs is $60,000. What journal entry does the company record at Year 1-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $30,000, Credit SAR Liability $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $25,000, Credit APIC-SARs $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $30,000, Credit APIC-SARs $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $25,000, Credit SAR Liability $25,000

questions.explanation: Equity-settled SARs are classified as equity instruments under ASC 718. Like equity-classified stock options, they are measured at grant-date fair value and not remeasured. Year 1 expense = $50,000 / 2 years = $25,000. The credit goes to APIC (equity), not to a liability. The current fair value of $60,000 is irrelevant for equity-classified awards — only the grant-date fair value of $50,000 is used.
questions.explanationJa: 持分決済型SARは持分に分類され、付与日公正価値で測定し再測定しない。Year 1費用 = $50,000 ÷ 2年 = $25,000。貸方はAPIC（持分）であり負債ではない。現在の公正価値$60,000は持分分類では無関係。

questions.stem: A company has 100,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $20. The average market price during the period is $50, and net income is $500,000. Weighted-average shares outstanding are 1,000,000. Using the treasury stock method, how many incremental shares are added to the diluted EPS denominator?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 100,000 shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: 40,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 60,000 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 0 shares (anti-dilutive)

questions.explanation: Under the treasury stock method (ASC 260-10-45-22): (1) Assume all 100,000 options are exercised, generating proceeds of 100,000 × $20 = $2,000,000. (2) The proceeds would repurchase $2,000,000 / $50 = 40,000 shares at the average market price. (3) Incremental shares = 100,000 issued - 40,000 repurchased = 60,000. Since the exercise price ($20) is less than the market price ($50), the options are dilutive and the 60,000 incremental shares increase the diluted EPS denominator.

questions.stem: Under ASC 718 (as amended by ASU 2018-07), how should share-based payments to nonemployees (such as consultants) be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the fair value of the goods or services received, measured at each reporting date

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, measured at each reporting date until the performance completion date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, using the same measurement principles as employee awards

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, measured at the grant date, with the performance completion date as the measurement date

questions.explanation: ASU 2018-07 simplified nonemployee share-based payment accounting by aligning it with the guidance for employees under ASC 718. Nonemployee awards are now measured at the grant-date fair value of the equity instruments, using the same fair value measurement principles as employee awards. The previous requirement to remeasure at each reporting date until the performance completion date was eliminated.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2018-07により、非従業員への株式報酬の会計処理が従業員と統一された。非従業員への報酬も付与日の持分商品の公正価値で測定し、従業員報酬と同じ測定原則を使用する。従前の各報告日における再測定（業績完了日まで）の要求は廃止された。
